text: !” “I don’t know what the fuck you’re talking about, Miss Brooks , but — ” “Cheyenne! All those people. You left us to sift through the fucking Dust, trying to find enough shit to earn a few bites of food from that bastard when this whole time you had food and shelter and medicine here. You have protection here !” She didn’t realize that she’d stood up and stepped toward Rodriguez until Ronin’s hands settled on her shoulders and stopped her forward movement . Rodriguez didn’t back away, didn’t break eye contact. “You’ve been through a lot, and I can’t pretend I know what it’s like .” “And you did nothing! My sister is dead, hundreds of people are dead, and you might as well be responsible for it. You let that monster run free, while you live here in luxury .” “We work for everything we have down here. Every. Fucking. Thing. Every time someone eats a piece of fruit,” his eyes dropped to the crushed apricot in her trembling fist, “we have to worry about whether we’ll be able to produce another to take its place. Every time someone needs medical attention, there’s a chance we’ll never be able to replace the supplies used to care for them. We have a lot of our own people to worry about right here, Miss Brooks. We’re not responsible for the rest of the damned world .” “Fuck you!” she screamed, chest heaving. Agony pierced her entire being. Black spots dotted her vision . “ Fuck …” “That’s enough, dammit!” Nancy rushed forward . Lara’s legs gave out, but Ronin was there, taking her sagging weight into his arms. He carried her to the bed and laid her down carefully . “You need to drink this now,” Nancy said. Something cool pressed to Lara’s lips . It smelled sharply of alcohol and something else; the sleep of the dead . “No. I don’t…” Lara swiped at it, but Nancy was quicker, moving the cup before it was knocked out of her hand . “Tilt her head back, and plug her nose .” Ronin’s hands followed Nancy’s orders, too strong to fight, but somehow gentle. Lara stared up at him, unable to believe the betrayal . “This will dull the pain and calm you, before you do any lasting harm to yourself,” he said, words oddly strained . Tears stung her eyes, and she averted her gaze. The moment she opened her mouth to take a breath, Nancy poured the foul liquid in, and covered Lara’s lips to prevent her from spitting it out . With no other choice, Lara swallowed . “She’s not going to get out of it,” Rodriguez said. “I expect to be notified when she’s well enough to be questioned, Doctor Cooper.” His footfalls were heavy as he walked away . “I’m sorry, Lara, I really am,” Nancy said, pursing her lips as she and Ronin removed their hands. “You need to rest. Once you’re stronger, you can berate Jack all you want, but as your doctor, I can’t allow you to hurt yourself.” She brushed a stray lock of hair back from Lara’s temple, glanced briefly at Ronin, and left . Ronin’s fingers slipped around Lara’s right hand, but she jerked it out of his hold . “ Don’t .” “I vowed to protect you, Lara Brooks,” he said, touching her chin and gently guiding her face toward him. She met his eyes before he leaned forward and brushed his lips over her forehead. “I’m not going to fail to uphold that vow again .” Chapter Thirty R onin leaned against a concrete wall in the main chamber, watching the activity around him. He’d yet to determine the number of inhabitants with any certainty — two hundred and fifteen unique faces were catalogued in his memory, but there were many places he hadn’t accessed . The base was well-maintained, but it was lived-in. Total cleanliness wasn’t possible here. People worked, interacted, and played in the chambers and corridors. Conversations were frequent, and typically amiable. Children’s laughter echoed off the walls, nearly swallowed up in the din of activity . Cheyenne was in stark contrast to this place. The buildings and grounds of the Bot District were impeccably maintained, despite limited resources. Every house had intact windows, freshly-painted siding, and neatly trimmed lawns, hedges, and trees. The streets were free of debris. But there was no laughter, no warmth. Many of the residences within the wall were occupied, but the Bot District was as cold and silent as a tomb . The slums represented the opposite extreme. The humans there lived in squalor, in ramshackle buildings pieced together from whatever scrap was available. It was a place filled with people, yet somehow devoid of life. People sat near their doors and stared with dead eyes at passersby, surviving only because doing so was habit . Laughter caught Ronin’s attention now. He shifted his optics to a nearby group — ten children following a tall, box-torso bot. One of the children had written on the bot’s back with a piece of chalk . KICK ME At their laughter, the bot halted. The children stopped, too, failing to stifle their giggles with their raised hands. Slowly, the bot’s head turned around to look down at them with round eyes . “The tone of your amusement indicates mischief. Please explain so we may return to class immediately .” Ronin couldn’t be sure why — was it the way the bot’s head turned, the tone of its voice, or how it looked with its head on backwards? — but the children burst into fits of laughter. The bot’s head remained in place as its body ponderously tottered around to face them . “Explain. Or I will be forced to issue detention slips to all of you .” Another bot — a sleeker model, but not a synth — stopped behind the teacher. Its head tilted, and after a moment’s pause, it
